Justification: The primary target users are individuals with mobility impairments, including spinal cord injuries, muscular dystrophy, cerebral palsy, multiple sclerosis, and age-related mobility decline.; Users with cognitive impairments will be accommodated through simplified controls, tactile feedback on all buttons, pre-programmed route options, and intuitive user interfaces. Specific design adaptations will be validated through user testing with individuals representing diverse cognitive spectrums.; Target user anthropometrics include a weight capacity up to 150 kg, and accommodation for users with varying heights and body types. Design will optimize for user independence, minimizing complex maneuvers or the need for external assistance.; User testing will focus on refining interface accessibility, safety alert clarity, and physical control ergonomics for users with cognitive impairments.

The wheelchair must provide reliable mobility on smooth indoor floors, paved outdoor pathways, and moderately uneven outdoor terrain (e.g., well-maintained grass, fine to medium gravel).; Mild inclines will be precisely defined as up to 10 degrees sustained. The wheelchair must be capable of ascending these inclines at a minimum speed of 3 km/h with maximum load.; A minimum 'on-the-spot' turning radius of less than 1.5 meters is critical for maneuverability in confined spaces.; The requirement for 5cm obstacle clearance is essential for user independence. This will be achieved through robust wheel and suspension designs utilizing specific high-strength, recyclable aluminum alloys. Feasibility will be confirmed during detailed design.; Minimum ground clearance will be 12 cm under the main chassis and critical components, ensuring navigation over varied terrain and small obstacles.

The electric wheelchair shall provide a minimum operational range of 25-30 kilometers on a single full charge, under specified test conditions (e.g., flat terrain, 75kg user, 6 km/h).; Battery technology will be Lithium Iron Phosphate (LiFePO4), with a targeted lifespan of at least 1000 charge cycles. A concrete end-of-life plan for battery packs will be established, including collection, recycling, or repurposing programs.; Full charge time will be no more than 8 hours. The charging system will be highly efficient, targeting >90% charge efficiency.; The BMS will include safety features and facilitate safe, easy removal of the battery pack for service and end-of-life processing. Battery pack design will be coupled with a clear take-back and recycling program.; The battery pack will be designed for secure, tool-less removal, with clear instructions for handling and return for recycling.

Seating system adjustments (lumbar support, seat depth, backrest angle) will utilize standardized ranges and mechanisms for reduced part complexity and manufacturing cost.; Cushioning will use high-density recycled foam and durable, washable, sustainably sourced natural fibers (e.g., hemp, organic cotton). Materials will be processed for easy separability for recycling, minimizing the use of glues and adhesives.; Armrests will be adjustable in height and width, designed as modular components for easier customization and replacement. Footrests will feature eco-friendly, non-slip surfaces and be adjustable in height and angle.; The joystick control will be ergonomically designed for intuitive and precise control, with options for left/right-hand operation and adjustable sensitivity. Tactile feedback will be incorporated on all controls.; Vibration and noise levels will be minimized through design and material selection, ensuring a pleasant user experience without compromising recyclability.

A robust braking system including electromagnetic brakes and a manual parking brake will be standard. All safety components will be designed for easy replacement, repair, and recyclability.; Anti-tip wheels will be standard, designed for effective operation on moderate inclines and when encountering obstacles.; Seatbelts and harnesses will be made from sustainable materials (e.g., recycled polyester) and designed for adjustability and ease of use, with consideration for users with limited dexterity.; Integrated LED lighting will enhance visibility. Materials used in lighting systems will be sourced responsibly and be recyclable.; An audible indicator (horn) will be included for alerting pedestrians and other users.; Emergency stop functionality will be clearly defined, integrated with motor control, and designed for easy replacement and end-of-life management.; The wheelchair will meet relevant international safety standards (e.g., ISO 7176 series, FDA regulations for medical devices), with compliance processes integrated from project outset.

The frame and structural components will be constructed from recycled aluminum alloys, specifying alloy types and quality control for recycled content. Composite structures will be avoided for structural elements due to recyclability issues.; Components will be selected for longevity, with expected lifespans for high-wear items (e.g., tires, joysticks, motors) defined. Mono-materials will be prioritized where possible.; Sealed bearings and motor enclosures will protect against dust and moisture ingress, utilizing materials with recyclability and minimal hazardous substance content in mind.; A modular design approach will facilitate easy maintenance, repair, and replacement. Modules will be connected using non-permanent fasteners (screws, durable clips) and minimal adhesives, avoiding glued assemblies. Specific examples of connection methods will be detailed.

Mandatory recycled content targets: >50% recycled aluminum for frame, >70% recycled plastic for non-structural parts. Other materials will be selected for recyclability and low embodied energy.; The design will aim for energy efficiency, targeting specific energy consumption metrics (e.g., Wh/km). Regenerative braking will be incorporated where feasible.; Detailed Design for Disassembly (DFD) principles will be implemented: specifying fastener types (screws, durable clips), avoiding permanent adhesives, and using mono-materials to facilitate material recovery. Material passports will track origins and recyclability.; Packaging will be minimized, made from recycled and recyclable materials, and designed for easy separation. Low-VOC or water-based paints and coatings will be used.; The product will be designed for longevity (target 10+ years lifespan) and repairability, functioning as a 'future resource bank' with readily available spare parts for at least 10 years post-production.

The wheelchair will feature a modern, sleek, and unobtrusive design that promotes user dignity. Paints and coatings will be low-VOC or water-based.; Color options will include muted, natural tones and customizable accents made from easily separable and recyclable materials. The design will prioritize intuitive operation, especially for users with cognitive impairments.; The user interface will be intuitive and easy to understand, with clear labeling and visual feedback. Customizable accents will not hinder material recovery.; The overall user experience, from setup to operation, will be positive and empowering, considering ease of cleaning and maintenance.; The design will avoid 'medical' connotations, aiming for a product users are proud to use.

The wheelchair will be designed for maintenance using standard tools, minimizing specialized tools. All replacement parts will be made from recycled content where feasible and designed for easy end-of-life processing.; Key components will be easily accessible for inspection and servicing, with design principles for disassembly using non-permanent fasteners and minimal adhesives.; A comprehensive user manual and service guide will be provided, detailing maintenance procedures and troubleshooting steps. Diagnostic capabilities will provide clear, actionable information for user-level troubleshooting.; Diagnostic capabilities will be integrated to assist technicians and users in identifying and resolving issues efficiently, reducing unnecessary service calls.; The modular design will allow for quick replacement of common wear-and-tear items, minimizing downtime and ensuring long-term parts availability.

The target retail price will be competitive within the accessible electric wheelchair market, aiming for a balance between advanced features and affordability. Cost-saving measures will not compromise durability, repairability, or recyclability.; Material and manufacturing choices will be optimized to control costs while prioritizing sustainability and circularity principles. Lifecycle cost analysis will incorporate environmental externalities.; The long-term cost of ownership will be emphasized, highlighting lower costs due to durability, repairability, and energy efficiency. Upfront cost considerations will not compromise circularity.; Modularity will support tiered feature sets, offering options without excessive complexity. This approach aids in managing cost and providing value.; Consideration will be given to potential for insurance reimbursement or governmental subsidies, with design choices supporting relevant certifications.

The product must comply with all relevant national and international regulations for medical devices and electric mobility aids, including safety standards (e.g., ISO 7176 series), EMC regulations (e.g., IEC 60601-1-2), and battery disposal regulations (e.g., WEEE).; Design will actively facilitate compliance with battery disposal regulations and promote responsible end-of-life management. Certification costs and timelines for international safety standards will be factored in from project outset.; Certification processes (e.g., CE marking, FDA clearance) will be pursued as required for target markets.; Clear labeling and documentation will be provided, ensuring compliance with all applicable laws and standards, including end-of-life management directives.

Future iterations may incorporate optional connectivity features, designed as modular add-on options or for future product generations. These components will be easily separable and recyclable.; Smartphone integration (Bluetooth/Wi-Fi) will allow for remote diagnostics, battery monitoring, and personalized control settings. Smart features will be designed with low-power modes and user control to minimize energy consumption.; GPS tracking will be considered for enhanced user safety, with components designed for easy replacement and recycling, avoiding proprietary connectors.; The design will allow for potential future upgrades without requiring a complete redesign, ensuring compatibility with future technologies.
